title: Fast contracted Clebsch--Gordan tensor products for equivariant graph neural networks
authors: Anton Bochkarev, Yury Lysogorskiy, Ralf Drautz
abstract: We present an $\mathcal{O}(L^3)$ algorithm for evaluating contracted Clebsch--Gordan tensor products in $\mathrm{O}(3)$-equivariant machine learning potentials at fixed Canonical Polyadic (CP) rank. Mapping the angular integral to a structured Gauss--Legendre and Fourier tensor-product grid decouples the radial channel contractions from the angular transforms. The antisymmetric parity-odd Clebsch--Gordan channels, unreachable by the symmetric pointwise product on a scalar $S^2$ grid, are recovered through the surface-curl pairing $\hat r \cdot [\nabla_{S^2} A \times \nabla_{S^2} B]$, the spherical Poisson bracket, which supplies the $L=1$ angular momentum on the grid while preserving rotational equivariance. The construction extends to parity-aware equivariant message passing in atomic-cluster-expansion-style architectures and is verified by direct numerical quadrature. The full uncontracted Clebsch--Gordan tensor product remains subject to the $\mathcal{O}(L^4)$ output-size lower bound. A benchmark shows wall-clock scaling empirically as $L^2$ across the practical $l_{\max}$ range. For the on-site contraction this is pre-asymptotic, giving way to $L^3$ at large $l_{\max}$. For message passing it is structural and the runtime is memory-bandwidth bound on $L^2$-sized grid tensors.
summary: This paper introduces SCALE-CG, an efficient algorithm for computing equivariant tensor products in machine learning potentials. It solves the problem of capturing parity-odd angular momentum channels while significantly reducing computational complexity from O(L^4) to O(L^3). This enables much larger angular momentum expansions in atomistic simulations.
main_problem: The computational bottleneck in O(3)-equivariant graph neural networks caused by the high scaling of Clebsch-Gordan tensor products and the inability of standard scalar grids to capture parity-odd channels.
main_result: The development of the SCALE-CG algorithm, which achieves O(L^3) complexity and uses a surface-curl identity to recover parity-odd channels, with empirical wall-clock scaling of O(L^2).
method: A structured tensor-product grid approach (Gauss-Legendre and Fourier) combined with CP decomposition and a surface-curl pairing (spherical Poisson bracket) to evaluate contracted tensor products.
model_or_system: Equivariant graph neural networks (GNNs) and atomistic machine learning potentials (such as the ACE-family) operating on O(3) irreducible representations with explicit parity.
key_observables: Clebsch-Gordan coefficients, parity-even and parity-odd channels, and computational scaling (wall-clock time vs. angular momentum L).
important_parameters: Maximum angular momentum (L), CP decomposition rank (C), number of radial channels (N), and parity (p).
main_assumptions: The O(L^3) scaling assumes a fixed Canonical Polyadic (CP) rank; the surface-curl construction for parity-odd channels vanishes if inputs are identical and mixing is symmetric.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows log-log plots of wall time versus L for on-site contraction and message-passing, comparing SCALE-CG against sparse direct implementations and theoretical scaling lines.
paper_structure: The paper introduces coupling operations, derives the surface-curl identity, implements O(L^4) and O(L^3) pipelines, and provides numerical validation and scaling benchmarks.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Forward-pass wall time vs. L = ℓmax + 1 for the on-site contraction Eq. (1) (left) and the message-passing coupling Eq. (2) (right). Blue: optimized sparse direct CG sum. Orange: SCALE-CG. Dashed grey: best-fit reference lines ∝L4
